---
name: "🗺️ Roadmap Proposal"
about: "Propose a strategic direction, a new horizon item, or challenge the current roadmap"
title: "[roadmap] <topic>"
labels: ["type: question", "needs: spec", "status: needs-triage"]
assignees: ["denisakp"]
---

> **This template is for strategic proposals** — things that would affect the product direction,
> not a single feature. For individual features, use the Feature Request template instead.
>
> Good examples: "We should add multi-location checks before going Cloud",
> "The Enterprise boundary between escalation and core feels wrong",
> "H3 should prioritise the Public API before multi-tenancy".

---

## Proposal Summary

<!-- One paragraph. What are you proposing and why does it matter for Ogoune's trajectory? -->



## Current State

<!-- What does the roadmap say today? What gap or tension are you identifying?
Reference specific horizon items if applicable (H1/H2/H3/H4). -->



## Proposed Change

<!-- What specifically should change, be added, be removed, or be reprioritised?
Be concrete: "Move Prometheus metrics from H2 to H1", "Gate multi-location checks behind Enterprise", etc. -->



## Strategic Rationale

<!-- Why is this the right move? Consider:
- Community adoption impact
- Open Core boundary (does this attract or alienate contributors?)
- Competitive positioning vs Uptime Kuma, UptimeRobot, Better Uptime
- Technical feasibility given the current Go/Vue 3 stack
-->



## Risks of Not Doing This

<!-- What happens to the product / community / commercial trajectory if we ignore this proposal? -->



## Risks of Doing This

<!-- Technical debt? Breaking changes? Community fragmentation? Scope creep? -->



## Dependencies

<!-- Does this proposal block or get blocked by other roadmap items? -->



## Success Metrics

<!-- How do we know this was the right call in 6 months?
Example: "50+ GitHub stars from new users citing Heartbeat monitoring", 
"First Enterprise customer using multi-tenancy", etc. -->



## References

<!-- Competitor features, community discussions, GitHub issues, relevant PRs, user feedback links. -->
